author: Jdub
content:
Whats up everyone!

I want to give a warm welcome to Daniel AKA <@568263613123395584>  

He will be sharing his premarket analysis, trade ideas, and doing trade reviews.

Daniel has been trading for over 5 plus years (Currently trading full-time for a living)

Daniel is a futures & options trader, primarily shifting his focus to futures in the past couple of years.

He’s been funded by many top prop firms with millions in funding capital and has had numerous payouts.

Daniel mainly trades ES and NQ and trades a very mechanical system using levels and liquidity.

I’ve personally been talking to Daniel for over 1 year now and I’ve seen his results and progression as a trader. 

He will help build the foundation for futures trading in our community! 

We will only continue to grow and provide as much value as possible. 

@everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
What’s up everyone! 👋

Super excited to be here! I’ve been trading for 5 years and went all-in on futures last year.

I’m based in Southern California, and my trading name is inspired by my childhood hero, Kobe Bryant—RIP to the GOAT! 🐍

What I love about futures is the ability to manage risk effectively without battling theta decay. My system uses a 1-candle stop loss, allowing for precision and tight risk control.

If you're a newer trader or working with limited capital, futures are the best way to go. They allow you to develop a strong system and gain real experience without high costs. Once things start clicking, you can leverage prop firms and scale to $10K+ per month.

I primarily trade the 4 major indices (ES, NQ, RTY, YM) but focus mostly on ES and NQ because they offer consistent moves and high-quality risk-reward setups almost daily.

My approach is level-to-level trading based on liquidity and market structure. I’m here to share my system and processes to help you build an edge, develop strong trading habits, and master your psychology. 

Big shoutout to Jack and Tony for the opportunity—let’s crush it together! 🫡 

@here

author: MambaTrades
content: Huge part of trading is knowing when to trade and when to walk away. If you are trading the indices it’s very important to walk away when you identify poor price action and chop especially on a Friday. Huge moves often lead to inside days like today. I personally remove myself from the screens so I can’t take a trade. It’s like going to a casino and staying at blackjack table. If you’re there you’ll convince yourself “one more’” so key is to step away. Thanks for attending my Friday ted talk 😆 @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content: Prime example of stepping away is key. If I stayed at the desk, I could have taken dumb trades, went on tilt, gave back all my weekly profits. Dont stay at the blackjack table, leave the casino when you’re up on the week. @here

author: MambaTrades
content:
Use the economic calendar to guide your trading bias for the week. 

FOMC generally leads to consolidation and small ranges that’s not worth your time and mental capital prior to the announcement. 

Why burn your confidence and energy when banks and smart money are waiting for the release? 

To be a successful trader you must do what the 90% of unsuccesful trader cannot which is wait. 90% of trading is waiting for your setups  in the right market conditions.

author: MambaTrades
content:
The indices have been extremely tough to trade the past 2-3 weeks outside of the CPI day. A lot of moves happened after hours due to news which leads to a lot of consolidation afterwards. I know professional traders with 8+ years struggling at the moment. 

Reminder to stay disciplined and focus on the long game. Trading is a marathon and you will have some drawdown weeks. Key thing to is keep your mental intact and not go on tilt or blow up your accounts at this time. I’m sized down and taking less trades until we get some clean price action. Journal, journal, journal to get better as a trader @here

author: MambaTrades
content:
One of my biggest issues the past 2-3 weeks of trading was being married to my daily bias and not being adaptive to change in price action and volume. 

For example, we've had a lot of gap downs and gap fills up and I kept trying to short the move up because I was married with the higher timeframe bias/narrative. Not good for this current market conditions with all the news, tariffs, and dip buyers. 

Moral of the story is be flexible and be willing to adapt your bias if price tells you a different story. 

Have a great trading day @here

author: MambaTrades
content:
Here's more on bias:

Have a directional bias, but be willing to drop or flip it if price action proves you wrong.

 Why Bias is Useful:
Gives you a framework for anticipating setups

Helps you plan risk, targets, and areas of interest

Keeps you focused on one side until invalidated

Why Rigid Bias Hurts:
Markets constantly evolve — they don’t care about your plan

Clinging to bias blinds you to shifts (e.g., failed breakdown turning into a reversal)

Most big losses come from fighting price

The Middle Path:
Start each day with a bias. Monitor for invalidation. Flip or stand down if the market shifts.

Use “If/Then” thinking:

“If price holds below this level, I’m bearish. But if it reclaims and shows strength, I’ll drop the bias or even go long.”

author: MambaTrades
content:
Valid reasons for not trading pre cpi

Uncertainty
Markets often chop or fake breakout the day before CPI as participants position themselves.

Liquidity traps
Algorithms frequently run both sides (highs and lows) to trap traders ahead of CPI.

Low-quality moves
Big players wait for CPI to commit → the day before is often low conviction, low follow-through.

author: MambaTrades
content:
Futures update. 

If you have fomo thinking “I could have or I should have shorted NQ to previous day close” DON’T unless it was part of your original morning plan. 

Coulda woulda shoulda does nothing for you. Following your morning plan and rules consistently does. No need to have fomo on a unplanned move. 

The market does what it wants to do. You will miss moves everyday. Your job is to react, manage risk, and follow your daily plan @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
Futures update @everyone 

Patience will be key this week. Monday and Tuesday could be slow with no red folder news. I think we will have better action Wednesday-Friday.

Every Monday have the mindset of taking 0 trades unless there is a clear setup. Worst thing you can do is start the week of in a large deficit. The plan for tomorrow is to target London high (once established), Asia high, or previous day high. If we are chopping on the hourly and 4hr it’s probably best to just sit on hands and wait till Tuesday to Friday. 

Reminder, you do not need to trade every day especially on Monday. You only need 2-4 good quality trades per week to be really successful in futures

content:
if you can avoid days like this, preserve your capital (especially your mental capital), and trade the high quality days like yesterday, Thursday, and Friday, you will be profitable a lot sooner. 

Trading is not that hard if you can avoid range days and trade the trend days. 90% of traders can't avoid the chop days due to FOMO, greed, and rushing the process to make money. The more you rush the process, the further you will push yourself away from the goal

author: MambaTrades
content:
Going to call it here as NQ and ES is stuck inside its range now. It's going to be the last day for me as the rest of the year should be low volume with tends to lead to a lot of chop and manipulation.

Take the rest of the year to reflect and create a list of focus on for 2026. This could be overtrading, risk management, sizing, techincals, psychology, etc. Put it on a post it note and focus on that each day instead of making money.

 If you solely focus on following your rules, manage your emotions, and taking great trades, there is no way you shouldn't be profitable in 2026. Good process and psychology is what will make you profitable. 

Have a great rest of the year everyone! Let's crush it for 2026 @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
If you're not backtesting, make it a goal to start backtesting in 2026. I recommend 1-2 years of data so you will become better at pattern recognition and executions in the live markets. 

There is a reason why pilots fly on simulations and doctors go to residency before becoming real doctors. Same for traders, you need to build reps so you're confident and strong in executions in real time.

The markets is going to be slow the next week so my goal is to get about 6-12 months of backtesting and journaling to get ready for 2026. 

What are you going to do while the markets are slow? Put in the work if you want to succeed in a craft that 90% fail @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
There are 248 trading days left in the year. 

If you missed a trade, feel fomo, tilt, or took a loss - walk away. The market isn’t going anywhere. You don’t need to make money today. 

Focus on building the good habits for 2026. 1-2 high quality trades if presented. Walking away after a loss. No revenge trading. If you missed a trade and hit its targets, walk away. 

Focus on the process and the money will follow @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
Reminder that this is not normal price action. We expanded 3406 points (15% on NQ) in 11 trading days. NQ will sometimes move that much in 6-12 months. The same move down took over 2 months of price actions. 

Don't get too overconfident and start sizing up because the market will humble you. Protect your capital and profits especially if you're up the past 2 weeks. After a huge distribution move, the market will accumulate and range eventually.

Remain humble, follow your plan daily, and don't get too cocky  because that will lead to overtrading and oversizing when the market starts to slow down/range. For now, ride the trend @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
Quick trading rant:

Huge overnight move generally lead to consolidation and Fridays are generally lower probability for futures and I believe options too 

If you trade Fridays have tighter rules so you don’t give back all profits or do anything detrimental to your account. 

For futures i generally like to focus on Tuesday-Thursday to put on risk and only take A+ setups on Monday and Friday. 

Umar Ashraf said there’s about 10 tradable days in a month and 6 very high probability. 

That means naturally you need to not trade on half the days 

Focus on trading less and putting more size on the easy days and your trading equity curve will go up 🚀 @everyone
